topic: A person unboxing the latest iPhone 16 Pro in Desert Titanium on a wooden table, front view.
raw_generation:
{
  "script": "Shot 1: Wide shot — a clean wooden table bathed in warm natural light. A hand enters from the left and places a sealed iPhone 16 Pro box labeled Desert Titanium on the table, with the front of the box facing the camera.

Shot 2: Close-up on the lid as the seal is gently lifted. The box opens to reveal the crisp white tray and the Desert Titanium phone sitting neatly inside, the front face waiting for the reveal.

Shot 3: The phone is carefully lifted out and placed face-up on the wooden surface. The Desert Titanium finish catches the light, showcasing its matte texture and smooth edges while the screen remains off for now.

Shot 4: The box is opened fully; a USB-C cable and quick-start guides are slid into view and laid neatly to the side.

Shot 5: The presenter picks up the phone again, powers it on with a press of the side button, and the front screen comes to life in a bright, clean display.

Shot 6: Close-up on the front of the device — the glass, bezels, and camera cutout at the top in front view — as the screen shows a simple welcome screen and the familiar liquid-smooth animation.

Shot 7: The phone is positioned back on the table, facing the camera. The Desert Titanium color and the front-facing profile are clearly visible as the presenter gives a quick nod.

Shot 8: Final shot fades in with a call-to-action: "Thanks for watching. Subscribe for a full review of the iPhone 16 Pro in Desert Titanium."",
  "narration": "Today we're unboxing the latest iPhone 16 Pro in Desert Titanium. We start with a front-view setup on a warm wooden table to keep the look clean and premium.

First, we crack open the box and reveal the phone nestled in its tray against the stark white interior. The Desert Titanium finish stands out with a soft, matte texture that feels premium in hand.

We lift the device, placing it face-up so you can appreciate the front design—the glass, the bezel, and the camera area at the top all neatly aligned in front view.

Alongside the phone, you’ll find the usual essentials: a USB-C cable and the quick-start guides, neatly arranged and ready for setup.

Powering the device on, the front screen lights up with a clean welcome, giving us a first look at the display and the front-facing details that define the design.

From this front-view unboxing, the Desert Titanium color and the refined build come across clearly. This is our first look at the iPhone 16 Pro, and we’ll bring you a full hands-on review soon. Thanks for watching." 
}

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1: Wide shot — a clean wooden table bathed in warm natural light. A hand enters from the left and places a sealed iPhone 16 Pro box labeled Desert Titanium on the table, with the front of the box facing the camera.

Shot 2: Close-up on the lid as the seal is gently lifted. The box opens to reveal the crisp white tray and the Desert Titanium phone sitting neatly inside, the front face waiting for the reveal.

Shot 3: The phone is carefully lifted out and placed face-up on the wooden surface. The Desert Titanium finish catches the light, showcasing its matte texture and smooth edges while the screen remains off for now.

Shot 4: The box is opened fully; a USB-C cable and quick-start guides are slid into view and laid neatly to the side.

Shot 5: The presenter picks up the phone again, powers it on with a press of the side button, and the front screen comes to life in a bright, clean display.

Shot 6: Close-up on the front of the device — the glass, bezels, and camera cutout at the top in front view — as the screen shows a simple welcome screen and the familiar liquid-smooth animation.

Shot 7: The phone is positioned back on the table, facing the camera. The Desert Titanium color and the front-facing profile are clearly visible as the presenter gives a quick nod.

Shot 8: Final shot fades in with a call-to-action: "Thanks for watching. Subscribe for a full review of the iPhone 16 Pro in Desert Titanium."
parsed_generation.narration:
Today we're unboxing the latest iPhone 16 Pro in Desert Titanium. We start with a front-view setup on a warm wooden table to keep the look clean and premium.

First, we crack open the box and reveal the phone nestled in its tray against the stark white interior. The Desert Titanium finish stands out with a soft, matte texture that feels premium in hand.

We lift the device, placing it face-up so you can appreciate the front design—the glass, the bezel, and the camera area at the top all neatly aligned in front view.

Alongside the phone, you’ll find the usual essentials: a USB-C cable and the quick-start guides, neatly arranged and ready for setup.

Powering the device on, the front screen lights up with a clean welcome, giving us a first look at the display and the front-facing details that define the design.

From this front-view unboxing, the Desert Titanium color and the refined build come across clearly. This is our first look at the iPhone 16 Pro, and we’ll bring you a full hands-on review soon. Thanks for watching.

feedback:
While the piece captures the unboxing on a wooden table and Desert Titanium iPhone 16 Pro, it diverges from the gold standard by not using bracketed timestamps or explicit timestamped segments. To better align, structure shots with [00:00-00:02] style timestamps, include a top-down/box-opening angle, add a macro peel shot of the screen, and specify tighter camera angles per shot to mirror the reference. Also maintain a concise, cinematic prompt style.
Failed checks: example_alignment
strengths: Front-view emphasis on Desert Titanium and clear device reveal.
Cohesive unboxing sequence from box to powered-on display.
fixes: Adopt timestamped shot structure with precise [00:00-00:02] format.
Incorporate at least one macro peel shot and top-down/side-angle variations to mirror gold standard.
